name: aligned_zero_size_vacuous
reason: If size==0 satisfies alignment, the spec fails to guard against division-by-zero, letting any address appear "aligned to 0"

name: aligned_odd_to_even
reason: 3 is not a multiple of 2; if provable, the modular-arithmetic check is fundamentally broken

name: aligned_implies_zero_addr
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_implies_zero_addr(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4096),
    ensures
        addr == 0nat,
{
}
reason: Many nonzero addresses (e.g. 4096, 8192) are page-aligned; if the spec collapses them all to zero, it is far too restrictive

name: aligned_sum_off_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_sum_off_by_one(a: nat, b: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s > 1,
        aligned(a, s),
        aligned(b, s),
    ensures
        aligned(a + b + 1, s),
{
}
reason: Adding 1 to a sum of two aligned values must break alignment for any size>1; provability would mean the spec ignores offsets

name: aligned_cross_size
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_cross_size(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
        aligned(addr, 2),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 3),
{
}
reason: Alignment to 2 does not imply alignment to 3 (e.g. addr==4); if provable, the spec conflates coprime alignment classes
